questions.question: During which stage of sleep do delta waves predominate, indicating the deepest level of sleep?
questions.explanation: NREM stage 3 (slow-wave sleep) is characterized by predominantly delta waves — slow, high-amplitude brain waves. This is the deepest stage of sleep, during which physical restoration occurs and it is most difficult to wake someone.

questions.question: As the night progresses, how does the composition of each sleep cycle change?

options.text: Later cycles contain more deep NREM stage 3 sleep and less REM sleep
Later cycles contain longer periods of REM sleep and less deep NREM sleep
All cycles throughout the night have identical proportions of each stage
Later cycles skip NREM stages entirely and consist only of REM sleep

questions.explanation: Early sleep cycles emphasize deep NREM stage 3 sleep (for physical restoration), while later cycles contain progressively longer REM periods (for memory consolidation and emotional processing). This is why cutting sleep short disproportionately reduces REM sleep.
